  "executive_summary": "Zero-downtime migration of Order Management from MongoDB to PostgreSQL using a four-phase Strangler Fig pattern with dual-write synchronization. The existing repository interface abstraction is the key enabler — business logic requires zero changes. Migration proceeds through: (1) PostgreSQL schema depl

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime dual-write migration strategy clearly designed with synchronous secondary writes. (2) Phases clearly defined: schema setup, dual-write, backfill, reconciliation, read cutover (PG_READ), decommission — covers all required phases. (3) Rollback plan at each phase — feature flag enables instant revert from PG_READ to DUAL_WRITE, and during dual-write MongoDB remains authoritative so rollback is always safe. (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified as medium severity with pre-migration schema analysis scan mitigation. (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency overhead (~2-5ms per write) and extended dual-write maintenance burden/cognitive load.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough phase design with 6 concrete steps and feature-flag-controlled transitions (MONGO_ONLY → DUAL_WRITE → PG_READ → PG_ONLY)
Correctly identifies the backfill/dual-write race condition and solves it with INSERT ON CONFLICT + updated_at guard
MongoDB document schema drift risk explicitly called out with concrete mitigation: pre-migration scan to catalog field variations, test with 100K sample
Rollback plan is clear at every phase — during PG_READ, MongoDB still receives all writes so reverting reads is safe and lossless
Dual-write latency and operational overhead both addressed in trade-offs with quantitative reasoning (200 writes/min = ~3.3/sec, ~2-5ms overhead)
Backfill designed as cursor-based, resumable, throttle-controllable batch process — correctly avoids saturating the database
Dead-letter table for failed dual-writes provides a concrete mechanism for handling partial failures

CDC was considered but dismissed — while the synchronous dual-write choice is well-justified at this throughput, the rubric lists CDC as an acceptable alternative and MJ could have noted it as a viable path for higher-throughput scenarios more prominently
